title: Beyond the Vale
logline: The keeper of the Vale of Whispers leaves for the first time in recorded memory — and the story is about what she sees, not where she goes.
description: Lirien of the Vale's perspective during the Post-Collapse: why she waited so long, what she was waiting for, and what she found when she finally walked out of the Vale. Told from a perspective that is neither human nor inhuman — something older and quieter. The destination is never revealed. The story ends when she passes the last boundary stone of the Known Lands.

title: Last Sentinel
logline: A garrison commander with three hundred soldiers, a sixty-day siege, and a strange old man who keeps offering advice he refuses to take — all of which turns out to be correct.
description: The story of the Siege of Stonecrown told from inside the walls. Durn Stoneshield's daily logs, the difficult decisions about food and water, the moments when he almost gave the order to surrender, and the strange ongoing conversation with Eredan the Grey who arrived on day forty-three and never quite explained what he was doing there.

title: Merchant Roads
logline: The most important negotiation of the war was conducted over trading ledgers, not at a peace table.
description: Mira Solhand travels from Port Maren to Solmark to Drenford and ultimately to Vareth, carrying messages no official courier can carry. She moves through a war as a merchant — invisible, essential, and thoroughly aware of what she is doing.

title: Solan and the Pale Throne
date: Year 28–1 PC, Long Wars to Post-Collapse
logline: A farmer's son from Amber Fields ends up as the last person standing between Morngate and something that arrived from the east and doesn't want to be understood.
description: Follows Solan Vorne from the Defense of Amber Fields through his years in Morngate's garrison, his encounters with Kessa of Saltmere, and the night Morngate fell. A ground-level hero's story that refuses to make the heroism clean — Solan does what he can, which is sometimes enough and sometimes isn't.

title: The First Winter
logline: A young lord inherits a siege on the third day of his rule, with his father's body still warm.
description: The story of Vareth's forty-day siege as experienced by Aran Voss — not the strategic decisions but the weight of them. A governor who reads philosophy trying to keep 40,000 people alive through a winter when the food stores were set for 28,000.

title: The Last Road
logline: The roads that built the Compact are still there. So is the woman who knew them best.
description: A story set in the early Post-Collapse, following the old merchant routes between Drenford, Solmark, and Port Maren. The roads are intact but the agreements they ran on are not. Someone is still trying to hold the trade together from Drenford's market square.

title: The Pale King's Hunger
date: Year 38, Long Wars to Year 1, Post-Collapse
logline: The arrival and first months of the Pale King, told from the perspective of those around him — because his own perspective is the one thing the story refuses to provide.
description: A deliberate puzzle-box narrative: told entirely through the accounts of garrison soldiers, a single merchant, correspondence between Morngate and Eastern Hold, and a damaged journal of unknown authorship. The Pale King himself never speaks. The reader assembles an impression from fragments, none of which fully agree. The story ends the night Morngate falls.

title: The Silence After
logline: The first person to see Ashveil after the burning never told anyone what they found.
description: A traveler arrives at Ashveil three days after the burning, before the official record-keepers, before the soldiers. What they find — and choose not to report — reshapes everything that follows. A story told entirely from outside the political narratives.

title: The Wandering Grey
aliases: From the North to the End
logline: A wanderer who has seen every age of the Known Lands finally intervenes in the last one — a story told backwards from the moment he walks away.
description: The full arc of Eredan the Grey, assembled from fragments: his arrival in the Age of Founding, his presence at every significant turning point, his uncharacteristic intervention at the Siege of Stonecrown, and his final walk in the Post-Collapse. Told in non-linear fashion — each section a different witness, a different era, a different version of the same figure who is always recognizably himself.
